inputs.name: accessibleDescription
inputs.description: Defines the accessible description of the component.

inputs.name: accessibleDescriptionRef
inputs.description: Receives id(or many ids) of the elements that describe the textarea.

inputs.name: accessibleName
inputs.description: Defines the accessible ARIA name of the component.

inputs.name: accessibleNameRef
inputs.description: Receives id(or many ids) of the elements that label the textarea.

inputs.name: disabled
inputs.description:
Indicates whether the user can interact with the component or not.

*Note:** A disabled component is completely noninteractive.

inputs.name: growing
inputs.description: Enables the component to automatically grow and shrink dynamically with its content.

inputs.name: growingMaxRows
inputs.description: Defines the maximum number of rows that the component can grow.

inputs.name: maxlength
inputs.description: Defines the maximum number of characters that the `value` can have.

inputs.name: name
inputs.description:
Determines the name by which the component will be identified upon submission in an HTML form.

*Note:** This property is only applicable within the context of an HTML Form element.

inputs.name: placeholder
inputs.description: Defines a short hint intended to aid the user with data entry when the component has no value.

inputs.name: readonly
inputs.description:
Defines whether the component is read-only.

*Note:** A read-only component is not editable,
but still provides visual feedback upon user interaction.

inputs.name: required
inputs.description: Defines whether the component is required.

inputs.name: rows
inputs.description:
Defines the number of visible text rows for the component.

*Notes:**

- If the `growing` property is enabled, this property defines the minimum rows to be displayed
in the textarea.
- The CSS `height` property wins over the `rows` property, if both are set.

inputs.name: showExceededText
inputs.description:
Determines whether the characters exceeding the maximum allowed character count are visible
in the component.

If set to `false`, the user is not allowed to enter more characters than what is set in the
`maxlength` property.
If set to `true` the characters exceeding the `maxlength` value are selected on
paste and the counter below the component displays their number.

inputs.name: value
inputs.description: Defines the value of the component.

inputs.name: valueState
inputs.description:
Defines the value state of the component.

*Note:** If `maxlength` property is set,
the component turns into "Critical" state once the characters exceeds the limit.
In this case, only the "Negative" state is considered and can be applied.

outputs.name: ui5Change
outputs.description: Fired when the text has changed and the focus leaves the component.

outputs.name: ui5Input
outputs.description:
Fired when the value of the component changes at each keystroke or when
something is pasted.

outputs.name: ui5Select
outputs.description: Fired when some text has been selected.
